title: Judge Calibration Tracking - Design Review

findings.finding: Calibration measurement sound and correctly implements expected vs actual accuracy
findings.detail: Buckets (0.5-0.6 through 0.95-1.0) use bucket midpoint as expected_accuracy. Calibration_error = expected - actual correctly flags overconfidence (positive) and underconfidence (negative).

findings.finding: Disagreement rate accurately measures judge vs ground truth
findings.detail: Algorithm counts decisions where _check_decision_correctness returns False, dividing by total. Fallback hierarchy: eval_result > ground_truth_correct > assume_correct. Fail-closed on missing eval.

findings.finding: Trust score formula justified and multi-dimensional
findings.detail: trust_score = (1 - disagreement_rate) * (1 - avg_calibration_error) combines accuracy (disagreement) and calibration. Clamps to [0, 1]. Fails closed (returns 0) on exception.

measure_calibration returns empty list if no decisions. compute_trust_score defaults to 0 on error. get_judge_disagreement_report returns minimal report if no decisions. All error_artifacts emitted immutably.

1) Overconfident judge (expected > actual, is_miscalibrated=True). 2) Underconfident judge (expected < actual, is_miscalibrated=True). 3) Trend detection (rising disagreement). 4) Empty data (empty list returned). 5) Error handling (error_artifact emitted on exception).
All artifact_store.put() calls include immutable=True flag. Records cannot be modified after creation. Immutability enforced by storage layer.
Searched for: enforcement, override, orchestration, closure, escalation, re-execution. All clean.

approval_summary: Phase 27 (Judge Calibration Tracking) design review APPROVED. Calibration measurement sound, disagreement detection accurate, trust score formula justified, fail-closed on errors, adversarial scenarios covered, immutability enforced, vocabulary safe.
